name: First Person Tennis - The Real Tennis Simulator

reviews.review_description:
I use the training mode to practice for real tennis. Older versions felt more realistic and useful for actual training.
After the recent updates, especially 5.3.2, shots feel too light and forgiving, with less realistic timing and ball contact.
Please consider adding a classic/simulation physics mode with less assistance.

developer_response.body: Older physics had more assistance. Now they are much more realistic. Completely zero assistance.

reviews.review_description: Amazing feel so real love it and play it everyday. I would suggest updating the crowd shouts as are repeating. I would blur the people more to focus more detail on the court itself . I think slow moving clouds would add to the experience plus weather variations for courts. But overall just excellent..
I played 1 game and decided to quit. No matter how hard I hit it, it would not go over the net. I rlly wished it would work properly, seemed like a good game, and I’m glad I got it from Horizon Plus

developer_response.body: You simply need to raise the trajectory by hitting the ball with the racket face angled more upward. If you want maximum realism, you can select Simulation mode, but obviously you need to know how to play tennis.

reviews.review_title: Maybe the hardware needs to improve
reviews.review_description: That’s my guess … it doesn’t handle slice shots correctly or bump shots imho. Better than the other tennis games in here.  Using the Wilson VR grip which is legit … but one with force feedback would be keen .  Meta4 will hopefully be a large jump in processing power

reviews.review_title: Please make it faster after faults
reviews.review_description:
Upd I mean in singleplayer

Please add an option to start next serve after faults immediately. It is really annoying to wait all these animations complete.

developer_response.body: It’s not possible because you have to wait for your opponent to be ready. The same thing happens in real life. Take your time to focus on the next point.

reviews.review_title: The most accurate simulation of tennis
reviews.review_description: This is the most accurate representation of real tennis available given current VR technology. I'm a former player and I've tried most tennis games. If accuracy is what you want, this is the one.

Today again i had the same issue, lagging when playing, loose point in the middle of the game. Last time developer told me to remove my post which i did!! There is no excuse about that, my internet connection is very good. I don't have any issue at all.
But today i saw something very very bad and not secured at all, bringing the game boring.
I stupid player keep on changing his account as he knows he or she is very good. He let me win the first match and then he start playing very well. This stupid fellow wants me to drop point as his point is always 1400 and when i loose i lost approximately 30 points as he or she is very good. Why you don't play with someone in the same level like you? It's my problem to play with players i want to play. Why changing accounts.
Here are the accounts: nicyoshat, bgg83, sampras98, pocitoia, bernad3, christian7, guti, booooo7 and so many more.  I'm sure it's the same person as i soon as i quit the game, i get immediately an invite to play. And i'm sure the developer will know by the ip address. But i know also he won't care about it!!!
And i know this does not happen to be only. 
This is ridiculous how people are so cheap. May be at the end of the day, they need affection!!
Discouraging!!!!

developer_response.body: Contact me on Discord or at mikorigames@gmail.com so i can restore your points

reviews.review_description:
First off I love and enjoy this game! But, is it me ? The color or imaging looks a little fuzzy or distorted?  It’s not a clear. Also, playing multiplayer is just not as cooperative.  Overall, I still enjoy the game 
￼￼￼

developer_response.body: You can increase the graphics in the main menu options.

reviews.review_description: I've been playing almost 3 years now and FPT is my favorite thing about VR and by far its best sports simulation. The game so close to real it replaces your memory of RL tennis. I actually enjoy FPT more than I ever did the real thing. For a long time the game was updated A LOT, which caused you to constantly have to re-adjust your game etc, but now it's found its groove and the updates simply add content (most recently doubles). The ball machine drone is fantastic. All sorts of way to train that you'd never be able to enjoy in the real world. FPT is work of art that keep getting better.

The physics seem great, but the teleport option keeps putting the ball either into my body or way outside my swing radius on both forehand and backhand side. The other VR tennis game I played is much better at putting you into a real swing position. The ball launcher in the training environment shoots too fast after a lob. This is a simulation, wait until the ball is struck or passed the player before sending out a new ball.

developer_response.body: All options are adjustable. The teleport distance from the ball can be changed. The ball machine shots are fully customizable. Contact me at mikorigames@gmail.com so I can help you with the settings.

reviews.review_title: this is how I get exercise
reviews.review_description:
thoroughly enjoying this, but...
the headset is like wearing blinders. I can't see where the ball hits (or misses) the racket. in the practice area, there is a messy graphic that shows the swing and the contact point but only when the ball hits the racket. I couldn't care less about the swing, I NEED TO SEE WHERE THE BALL HITS *OR MISSES* THE RACKET. please remove the MESSY swing graphic, that will reduce the programming so I can see the ONE important image. and, if possible, also show the image when the ball *misses* the racket.
thanks.

developer_response.body: Keep pressed the Meta button or recalibrate the room in the headset settings. Please change the feedback and contact me at mikorigames@gmail.com if you need help

reviews.review_description:
I have used this app regularly for a long time and it's great.
I thought it had malfunctioned the other day but I had toggled off a setting by mistake.
I contacted Meta first via WhatsApp chat and was dealt with in a prompt, efficient and friendly manner.
However, as it was a third party app I was advised to go directly to Mikori Games.
We conversed by email and the agent found the problem in no time.
I'm a very happy customer twice!

EDIT: RESPONSE. After posting this review, the developer banned me from the Discord server. This proves that he is not interested in feedback, only in silencing criticism.

The developer has admitted that the game does not track ball impact location. He stated: "It’s as if you always hit the ball in the center of the strings." You cannot claim "top players" are using "real technique" when the game artificially corrects every shot to be a perfect sweet-spot hit.
Pushing the stick forward triggers a momentum boost that results in unrealistic 100kmph defensive slices—something the dev admitted is possible in discord. As for the real-life coaches, he is talking about himself as he claims: "I'm a tennis coach and I studied physics of tennis so i know what happens."
--------------
At first I thought the game, because we are talking about a game and not about a simulator as the developer wants to advertise it as, is good for practicing your shots using the single player practice ball machine. You are inside a court instead of grabbing your racket at home and start shadow swinging. 

If you start playing multiplayer you will realize that the physics in this game are all over the place. The shot speeds are insane, hitting 110 kmph forehands and backhands with no effort and serves you haven't hit before so fast in your life.

The most bugged thing in this game though is the joystick movement. "High" rank players in the game are exploiting it, hitting shots using 0 technique. You can hit unrealistic angles and speeds just by moving forward with the joystick and hitting continental forehands without worrying about putting topspin on the ball. If you try to compete with them you will only ruin your real life tennis technique.

Definitely not worth 22€ for a game, again we are talking about a game and not a tennis simulator, where there is only one developer behind it, who doesn't want to take in consideration the community feedback and thinks he is always right about everything.

developer_response.body:
Hi, thanks for your detailed feedback.
I’d like to clarify a few important points about the physics and movement system, because some of the issues you mentioned are often misunderstood.

• The physics of the game are correct, but VR is not the real world
In VR you don’t have:
– the real weight of the ball at impact
– the inertia of a real racket slowing down your wrist and arm
These two factors naturally allow players to swing faster than in real life. This isn’t a “bug” — it’s simply a physical limitation of VR technology. Every VR tennis game deals with this. A tennis adapter helps this way.

• Top players are strong because of real-life technique
The strongest players in FPT are often high-level tennis players in reality. Their mechanics, timing, spacing and footwork allow them to generate speed and angles that are absolutely realistic when you understand their technique. There is nothing “unrealistic” about their strokes.

• Body movement does affect the shot
The game takes into account weight transfer, body rotation and momentum. Stepping into the ball gives extra speed just like in real tennis — and this is intentional. What some players interpret as an “exploit” is simply proper tennis technique applied correctly.

• I rely on feedback from real players and professionals
The tuning of the physics is based on the input of professional tennis players and real-life coaches. Their feedback has been fundamental for years. While I always listen to the community, I cannot base physics changes on assumptions from players who may not fully understand tennis mechanics.

• Improvements are always ongoing
I continuously refine the game, including movement realism and balance, but the goal is always to keep it as close to real tennis as VR technology allows.

reviews.review_title: One of the best out there!
reviews.review_description:
I’ve tried them all, and this one definitely stands out!

The developer is also very open to feedback. I had a great conversation with him and suggested a few improvements like making the ball more vibrant to help it stand out, and removing the side referees since most modern courts don’t use them anymore (perhaps replace the referees with Ball Kids?). I also mentioned adding an animated ceremony after winning a tournament; hopefully that’s coming.

Overall, this is easily one of the best options out there.

Many users have called this app 1-star, which basically means totally useless lemon. That is certainly not the case at all. Also, I needed to mention that I only play in Solo -> Arcade and Teleport mode. I did try other options and it didn't fit my style of play and also didn't like it as much.

New Update: Back to being the best game as of 2026. Support team helped me to fix the problem, it was a settings mess caused by me which they identified and fixed it. It is working again. If you are looking for endless tennis experience and workout, this is the best one out there.

Current Status, Jan 3, 2026: Latest Update screwed up everything. I can't win a single game in Advanced Mode. Ball always lands before the net and teleport option is not working making me miss all the shots. To Developer: Please revert the Updates to the Jun 2024 version. I had PAID for this and it is shame that it is not working as expected now.

APRIL 2024:
It worked very well as of Apr 2024, had. 5-Star review as below:
Very enjoyable and used almost everyday. Automatic Run feature is the best.

reviews.review_title: Best deal I have ever purchased.
reviews.review_description: Game plays great, offline. Some games I like aren't available offline. Thank you.

developer_response.body: Internet is not required

reviews.review_description:
So since I started playing this game, it was ruff at first. Then as I got use to how to win a game at practice. I really enjoy it. I do not look forward to that upgrade I've been reading about in the other reviews. If I can do without I would prefer that. So who ever the programmers are of this game, please take note of the reviews about upgrade because you are losing good customers though this upgrade. Either find out why it's causing problems or don't put it out. This is just my opinion 
Otherwise I like the game as is. No Upgrades.

developer_response.body: You already have that update. Updates are made to improve the game and by following players’ feedback (when they are correct).

reviews.review_description: I play this game on the Quest 3s using the Wilson/FormX racquet attachment in simulation mode. The rally physics are very accurate and realistic. However, serving is almost impossible — about two-thirds of the time, the ball either goes through the racquet or flies off in a weird direction. This doesn’t happen without the Wilson/FormX attachment, so I think the issue is that the controller goes out of the headset’s tracking range during the serve, causing it to lose position. That’s why I still gave 5 stars: it’s frustrating, but I don’t think the developer can really fix it.

developer_response.body: Yes, unfortunately it's an issue with the headset and Meta, which should improve tracking when the controller is behind the head. To improve tracking during the serve, you should try to keep your eyes on the ball at the moment of impact with the racket.

reviews.review_description:
The ultimate tennis simulator—unmatched now and likely for years to come. If you’ve ever stepped onto a court, this is a must-own. If you haven’t, get ready for a crash course in the sport itself. 

Great workout!

Heaps of fun, great way to jump in and have a 'rally' when you get a little spare time.

Haven't played arcade mode but on simulation I feel I'm getting a fair representation of tennis mechanics. The game seems to reward me for proper form and early preparation.

Such a great way to practice your swing, I feel like it's probably good for helping with timing and eye tracking too. Looking forward to seeing if practice in vr can translate to real life improvements.

reviews.review_title: still grt, too friendly to pushers imho
reviews.review_description:
update:  Still a fantastic game possibly best vr tennis game but no longer an  accurate tennis simulation as it allows playing in a negative style consistently and win. a player can dunk the ball at 75k halfway up the court and win the point at net! this is impossible in RLT. I get that different styles can get the job done, and nothing against any player however to be a simulation i would hope better realism. Of course there are limitations with technology which could cause this but not sure why a certain type of player is to be favored in this way. Just my opinion I still like the game but not 5 star level.

update: changed review after playing later versions this is now a very realistic tennis game esp in sim mode. if you think about the mechanics of tennis I.e. power, spin, position and ball physics it is staggering to think what has been achieved here. in addition to the gameplay the developer is kind enough to organize tournaments and is very responsive to suggestions and comments, very fair to all.
puts so called sims like ib cricket to shame which in spite of being built by major dev houses still have the same physics and realism issues.
I am sure for will only improve over time and add more features and realism..kudos!

…...........

Issues: 

1 Looses ball tracking when trying to get feet in the right position for shot, so you have to remain still and cannot get side-on for shots
2 Need more repeatable practice especially for serves in simulation mode, now is a bit of a lottery, no way to work out the correct service motion - tried this with a friend who is a school tennis coach and they had an impossible time trying to get their serves in!!
Otherwise this is a game with a lot of potential, if these issues are tweaked would have no issue giving a higher rating.

developer_response.body: Contact me at mikorigames@gmail.com or on Discord so i can help you. There are many ways to train your serve

reviews.review_description: i was so close to winning my first match but kept calling the things and i lost

developer_response.body: Can you describe your issue? Contact me at mikorigames@gmail.com

reviews.review_description: positioning is horrible, it will put you about 5 feet from the ball

developer_response.body: You should be on the blue square you see on the ground, that is the center of your room and the game moves you on the basis of that position. Check the movement deadzone settings too, they should be set at minimum. Contact me at mikorigames@gmail.com so i can help you.

reviews.review_description: This is clearly the best tennis VR app. Kudos to the developers for proactively updating the features to improve the experience.

This is not a tennis game that works within your boundary. Often time balls will be hit that you physically cannot reach without hitting into your wall. I play in a large space, but still, this game feels the need to make plays that are impossible for me to reach. This is VR, not a real court, so some adjustments to the app need to be made where the game can be reasonably challenging within the roomscale boundary set. I regret purchasing.

developer_response.body: The game can be played while standing still. The Teleport mode automatically moves you close to each ball so you can hit it. The Autorun mode, on the other hand, moves you at a realistic speed, meaning balls that are too far away can’t be reached — just like in real life. (Even if you move in the real world, you’ll never reach the ball, so always stay in the center of your play area for your safety). Please change the feedback and contact me if you need help with all the options available.

reviews.review_description:
I have changed my original review after spending more time with the ball machine to find out what I am doing wrong and can correct.
The volleying practice feels great. I definitely feel like I am on the court locating my volleys with precision and can actually use this on a real court.
I use a one handed backhand and this definitely rewards with good technique. Fortunately it is a natural shot for me.
The forehand is inconsistent for meso far in the game. I feel like I have to change my real life swing to make it work for the game. I don’t feel like I can hit the ball with intent as I would on a real court.
I do believe the margin of error is a more punishing than if you were playing real tennis.
Personally I would like to have more feel for lobs and serves. 
Maybe it’s a user error from me but I am not able to create a kick or slice with location nor any real power on serves.

developer_response.body: That’s because you’re not hitting the ball the same way with the same incoming shot, unless you’re playing in Arcade mode. Try Simulation mode instead. Join our Discord where you can chat and play with professional tennis players.

reviews.review_description: Sometimes, when you are playing, the ball is visibly in, and it is called out. You hit the net and it lands on the other side but the point is given to the other person. Specifically during big points this has happened to me and it’s very frustrating. please give this game, a makeover and a serious update.

developer_response.body: You can rise your hand and press trigger to call the hawkeye. The game can never be wrong about whether the ball is in or out. You can review the bounce in the replay.

reviews.review_description:
8/16/25 - The developer just gave me the solution to fix my problems below.  This is definitely a 5-star game and my favorite on the Meta Quest platform !

This is usually the first game that I play when I turn on my Quest 3.  But, since the last two updates, during practice mode when using the ball machine, every other ball that is launched by the ball machine goes into the net!  It didn’t do that in the past but now it does.  Can someone please update this game so that it will be back like it was before!?  It may have started doing that when they changed the sponsor to Wilson.

developer_response.body: Reset the shots list clicking the second button from the left in the drone settings. Contact me at mikorigames@gmail.com or on Discord if you need help. Please change the feedback. Thank you

reviews.review_description:
The most satisfying feeling upon contact out of the several tennis games I purchased, with the right amount of bounce and elasticity. Even better with the FormX handle after getting used to it. The grip is a size 2 and has a wrist strap, not shown in the picture. The weight feels just right when holding both controls, but slightly light when using one control. One handed backhand players can use the second control for tossing the ball in a natural manner, but two handers will likely have to resort to the trigger button on the racquet hand to serve(using only 1 control to play) The automatic toss from the racquet hand is great for seated players, and allows for serving if unable to stand. But it does not feel as natural when serving. Good to have for two handers and seated players.

I find I can play with spins, touch, and trajectories, shaping the ball in different ways. I can tell, just like in real tennis if my timing and shot feel clean and more likely to go in. I haven’t figured out how to program the ball machine though, as I find it quite confusing. I wish there were different racquets and strings to play with, that have different power, control, spin potential, launch angles, sizes, etc. The racquet seems to reward fuller swings, yet at the same time, it is sometimes difficult to deaden the ball at the net. Overall the hitting experience is much more forgiving than another simulation game I purchased.

Update: After a lot more play, I have stopped playing other tennis games I have purchased because they just don’t have the right feel compared to First Person Tennis. The other games do not feel satisfying with each hit. I can even do fun shots like a Michael Chang Underhand side spin serve when I lack ceiling space to do an overhead serve. Bounce the ball a few times with the toss hand to get a rhythm, then let it rip. It feels like you get back what you put into a shot, making it possible to hone a shot with practice . On my wish list for FPT: 1. Automatic Run Bias, for people who like to run around to hit a particular stroke. My brain often wants to hit a forehand, but the automatic run instead positions for a backhand. The joystick can override the automatic positioning, but it is much more difficult to use the joystick to get into position. 2. Customized Racquet Specs. I am used to a low powered racquet that has a lot of plow through feel at the net and chip shots. I really have to exaggerate chopping motions to get any kind of depth with the default racquet specs. The default is nice all around, but a little room for tweaking would be nice. 3. Ball machine presets to practice specific shots without needing to learn how to customize the ball machine settings. A more point and shoot experience versus full manual control for dumb dumbs like me.

FormX Update: I took off the Wilson grip to see what is underneath, and the handle is a nice firm and dense foam that has a very small amount of give, creating additional comfort, yet the feel of the controller vibration feedback is still easily felt through the handle and grip. Also, for one handed backhand players, the throat section that tapers outward has a very nice feel to it and is great for changing grips. A nice little touch. Also, racquet twirlers like me, need to either stop twirling, or make sure that the top of the controller can be seen by the headset , or else the virtual racquet gets disconnected from the handle. Bumping up the frame rate, and keeping strokes out in front also seem to help with tracking more accurately. And finally, the racquet handle is a little on the light side, but I think it is better to be light than too heavy, as adding weight with tungsten or lead strips seems like it would be easy. The buttcap doesn’t seem hollow or removable, so adding weight there might be more difficult.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the feedback on the Wilson/FormX adapter. We’re getting positive reviews from all players and it’s great to see that our work went in the right direction. Reach out to us on Discord if you need help with the ball machine, it has many settings to allow maximum customization, and that is just the simplest layout. As for how forgiving FPT is: FPT is not forgiving in simulation mode, it’s just that the physics in other games are very inaccurate, and often when you miss it’s not your fault but due to their flawed physics. Especially when moving the controller very quickly, FPT’s physics accuracy has no comparison. You can analyze the impact activating the swing analysis in the training section.

reviews.review_description: A fun, competitive workout that challenges you on every level. Simulation physics are the best a tennis game offers.

The game is good, but is at the same point bad, Because player are hitting serves like 150Km+ when is close to impossible to do that on quest vr game, NOT PC, when play from PC you can serve 150+, but if you do only qyest in close to impossible, at this point you are Handicap at the game

developer_response.body: The real physics are on the native version because if you use Quest on pc you have input lag, framerate losses etc and physics cannot work properly. Please change the feedback

reviews.review_description: Horrible game. There's so much glitches and the hit software sucks. Don't get it everybody.

developer_response.body: What issue do you have? The game is very solid. Contact me at mikorigames@gmail.com so i can help you with the setup of the headset

reviews.review_title: Probably the best tennis VR out there
reviews.review_description: I play tennis and I loved this game the minute I started playing it. My only issue is a user guide would be helpful especially with new features coming in every now and then. A few tweaks to the training settings and things start to go wrong. So you have to slowly try and figure it out yourself. Developer kindly give us a user guide with descriptions etc. Scrolling through Discord to find my specific issue takes a lot of time but it works.
I am using quest 2 but start drifting on its own while playing , also having boundary issues

developer_response.body: The stick of your controller has the drifting issue. It's a hardware problem, you can try to recalibrate the stick or increasing the deadzone. Contact me on Discord for more help.

reviews.review_description:
I'm probably in a small minority of people who use VR tennis exclusively to improve my real life tennis skills. I've owned this game for many years. The physics have been steadily improving, and with version 5.1 I can finally say it's good enough for serious training. I take a weekly lesson with a real coach, and this app allows me to realistically recreate many of the same drills with the ball machine. Hitting with the bot in rally mode also feels very close to the real thing. I switch back and forth between real and VR tennis and I no longer feel a jarring transition. It is more like playing with a different racket and a different person, but still the same game. 

I highly recommend this app for tennis training, but if you are serious about it's crucial to have two things: a racket attachment that makes the weight and balance similar to your real racket, and a space large enough to move without any form of artificial locomotion.

I’ve tried the other games and this has been the best tennis game on Meta as far as simulation goes. It’s not perfect, but I still think it deserves 5 stars and it continues to improve. I’ve been playing for several months on a regular basis and recently got a new patch with even more realistic physics and other improvements including many quality of life improvements which is awesome.

The developer continues to add new features and has said is working on adding doubles mode.

I do feel like the developers keep messing with the formula for the physics in this game, and it just leads to a lot of inconsistency overall. It’s like every time you boot up the game after an update, it plays worse than it did before. I just wish they could settle on the physics and let us play. Right now, it just feels like everything is off.

developer_response.body: The goal of every update is to make the physics increasingly realistic, and this pursuit will never stop, as this is a tennis simulator.

reviews.review_description: The game is really good but please please please make running faster. It feels like I’m  jogging to the ball when I need to sprint. If my opponent hits the ball really soft and in a corner then my character just walks to it. It doesn’t seem like it’s running

developer_response.body: The speed is set to a realistic value, deeply tested with professional tennis players, and it has been added a fatigue system. Please remove the feedback, if you want to chat you can join on Discord. https://discord.gg/first-person-tennis-the-real-tennis-simulator-442706345460891659

reviews.review_description: I’ve had this for quite a while now, and this helps me actually improve my form in real tennis. The game is extremely fun, and I always find myself sweating when done. The game has amazing physics, allowing you to aim, and control the spin on your shots. Overall, the best game for tennis out there when it comes to realism.

I don't understand all these players who criticize FPT!!
This game is incredibly realistic!
I've never played a better tennis game in the entire metastore, and yet I've tried them all.
I never got through the free trials of other tennis games because they were so pointless!
FPT is a true simulation game in the truest sense of the word, and anyone who doesn't understand what simulation is is a player who plays tennis on PlayStation, and in that case, the other tennis games in the metastore are what they need.
You have the choice between arcade and simulation, but some people aren't even capable of playing in arcade mode and find a way to criticize.
The developer is making sure to improve the game based on feedback from different players, which is remarkable.
This game is more and more incredible with each new update!
I thank the developer so much for its incredible work and for the real pleasure this game gives me! (Ivory)

reviews.review_title: Most realistic tennis in the store
reviews.review_description:
Good physics and realistic sensations in game.
As aspects to improve, I would like to see better animations and some more personality in the players, but I understand that it is something very expensive to achieve.
Still, it seems to me the best tennis game experience in the store. Good job!

Newest updated review:

Recent changes have restored the game to its former glory for me. Thank you! Loads of fun. Now if we can just address the rage-quitters….


Previous review:

Whatever changes have taken place between August 28 - October 8 have ruined the game for me. The game play - without my having changed anything to do with my technique (my serve was my greatest strength and is now my biggest weakness) is very different. It’s almost like more of the simulation type of play is now integrated into the arcade style. Used to play every day and now I couldn’t care to play at all.

FPT is not a real tennis simulator but a false title to sell!
It has ATP speed. You may quickly feel like Sinner under clostebol, but not learn real tennis.
Why? Because ball rebound on the racket reaches from below the grip to the head. The VR racket head detection is about 75x35cm, too wide compared to the real 32x25cm.
My YT video proof  that the ball can be hit below the hand under the displayed grip has been taken down by a copyright strike by Michele Laurello aka Mikori. 
You can hit the ball off-center with the frame, it still feels perfectly centered. When the ball can be hit hard with no care of when and how it touches the racket head, no eyes on the ball, it goes over the net more likely in arcade than simulation mode.
YT tournament videos show that games become long 10-30 repeated baseline rallies. It is not even fitness, as autorun will get you there, if not, joystick helps. Some racket adapters allow each stroke to fly effortless at 120. Even ATP players can't play this way.
Use trial & error to adjust to strange physics. Volley's are never decisive, awkward racket pull backs score at the net. Hard to reach baseline shots can be returned with a snap of the wrist. Adapt to a VR world with little resemblance to real tennis.
Friends get banned mentioning Tennis Esports, Court time tennis that require much focus because of a correct head sweet spot, very unforgiving, forcing the eye on the ball, which helps on a real court. When I try FPT again, wrong physics are so obvious in simulation mode. I can adapt but it's not tennis practice. It is harmful to my RL level.

Good online games are rare, seldom decent simulation players online. A closed community organizes plays among their own and share prize winnings. The same tournament winners can be seen on YT. If a challenger rises, like I did or another lately, he is targeted on a discord channel with no rules.

I even get threatened here for my honest review, 1 of 12%, so read my reviews for e-sport and  CTT.

developer_response.body: This player was banned from the Discord community and online play a long time ago due to aggressive behavior but continues to spam negative reviews out of revenge. There’s no point in arguing—just try the game and you’ll see that nothing he says is true. I’m sorry, but you will never play FPT again. Move on.

reviews.review_description: I have never played tennis so I have no idea if this game is anywhere near real tennis. But ….I absolutely love it ! I play often & have struggled with playing with some of the updates but it’s great game as far as I’m concerned. I recently had a problem with it. The developer responded immediately & solved the problem.

I have always wanted to try tennis in the real world, but alas, I do not have the time for it. I have tried other VR tennis games, and this one met all my criteria. It has given me a great workout session and really simulates a real tennis game compared to the rest. Thank you!

reviews.review_title: This is a very good tennis game!!!
reviews.review_description: Thanks for making a very fun simulator! You can get a great workout and enjoy winter training in the comfort of your own house. The developers have gotten back to me on some bugs and are very responsive. Thanks again for the great game!!

This was literally my favorite game in VR I raved about this game to everyone. NOW they changed it I can't hit a serve I can't return a ball I can't do anything. I literally can't beat the training. I might never play this game again wish I hadn't bought this game.

developer_response.body: Contact me at mikorigames@gmail.com

reviews.review_description:
As a lifelong tennis player,  I'm absolutely loving 
this simulation. (I refuse to call it a game) I only wish I had the physical space large enough to play it without automatic running mode, but it's really fun and a great workout anyway. The physics are spot on.  


I only have a few minor suggestions: I've never played a left-hander in any of my tournaments so it would be nice if you had that.  Also, I always serve first, which is not realistic, and there should be a virtual coin flip to decide that.  Finally,  it would be fun to create players (other than your own avatar) because I'd like to play against some of the all-time great players in tournament mode. 

All in all,  my favorite app on the Quest 3

UPDATE 2/22/25

I have been playing almost 6 days a week on simulation mode.  Just got the racket handle from Virtual Printality, and the realism is even better now. 

A few suggestions:I would like to see a quick button on the left controller that would take me to the net for a volley.  When I try to use the thumb stick to move forwards,  I always run sideways and take myself out of the middle of the court.  I would also like to see the AI opponents be able to put away volleys.  They usually just hit it right back to you,  giving you multiple chances to pass them,  and they never swing at lobs at all. I would love to see my other suggestions from my original review considered as well.  

Overall,  a fantastic simulation that is still my fave Quest game by far!

R

Reason I bought a quest. Have played basically every day since. 

There are 13 different arenas to play in, and it's amazing how different they feel. The physics feel very realistic, and I can already tell I'm going to be so much better next time I get out to a real court.

Heaps of settings to muck around with so you'll be fine even with a smaller play space. Dev is also super active on discord.

If you like tennis or even just racket sports, get this. Worth every penny.

reviews.review_title: I can play it for 2hours every day
reviews.review_description:
I can play this game for 2 hours every single day. It is hard to go outside to play in winter in some colder regions in China (p.s. I come from northern part of China) because the temperature can go very low. 

This game is a great alternative becasue I can play in a room scale. Feel like a real session. Have to give a 5 out of 5. Interesting game!

Update: I suddenly cannot play a real tennis on a court anymore because I think the weight of the racket on this game is too light compared to a real one. This gives me some uncomfortable feelings in a real field lol after a long winter season.

developer_response.body: You can use an tennis adapter for the controller if you want the weight of a real racket. Contact me at mikorigames@gmail.com if you need help.

reviews.review_description: I love this game - thank you for helping guide me to re-calibrate the settings with the B/Y in a match. For some reason the game became unplayable but now its back to normal. It feels like REAL physics, I play tons of racketsports -- this is real traditional feeling -- only thing you dont have to run after balls. Dont hesitate to buy this game if you LOVE TENNIS .. THANK YOU SUPPORT!!
Love the mixed reality tennis.  This is my favourite vr game.  In training, I wish you could review your stats, that's my only gripe.  But excellent game!
does anyone know any good extensions to use for this game for the quest 3?

reviews.review_title: Immersive Tennis Experience Like No Othe
reviews.review_description:
First Person Tennis is a truly impressive VR game that sets a new standard for sports simulations. From the moment you step onto the virtual court, the level of immersion is unmatched. The graphics are sharp, the environments are beautifully detailed, and the sound effects make you feel like you’re in a real match.
I Highly recommended for tennis fans and anyone looking for a top-notch VR sports experience!
Kudos to this game and developer. I have tried every tennis game, but this one has the most realistic physics.  It is as close to real life as possible. Keep up the good work.

This is brilliant.. its simple and graphics are not mind blowing , but the tennis physics are really really good..  I love playing tennis and this is excellent..  be warned it gets hot and sweaty with the game... Great job

I’ve not long purchased this and it is so excellent. I’ve been practising the serve, rallies and match practice matches and moved on to some tournaments around the world. My serve sucks at the moment and I’m finding less effort is a better result at the level I’m on but the shots feel really good and a realistic effort.
I’m thrilled as I was playing wheelchair tennis and have done for over 20years but my hands can no longer cope and I can’t even hold a racket, let alone push my chair. I am smiling now so much after only spending 45 minutes on this and I feel invigorated as I can manage to swing the controller and hit shots. Mind, I must take more care as I clonked my finger on the bed head..ouch. Love the different modes and the idea you can join in with others to play. Time enough for that as I need to practise more. 
Absolutely recommend especially if you have mobility issues. Felt I had a good work out😁😁

This game is just unplayable, full of bug in game, the teleport way of moving is made by a 5 yo kid  , the player is teleport out of the reach to get the ball , even the lowest difficulty is unwinable. Almost no calibration and bot are broken and made ball not even touchable half the Time. I will not pay that much while it's not correctly balance with good training exercise.

developer_response.body: Teleport automatically places you in the position where you can hit the ball, but it does so thinking you are always within the blue box you see on the ground, which represents the center of your room. This ensures you won’t bump into the walls. Make sure you stay within that square. If you need help with the many game options, feel free to contact me on Discord or at mikorigames@gmail.com.

reviews.review_title: PERFECT tennis physics and tons of fun
reviews.review_description:
RACKET HANDLE UPDATE: 

I enjoy playing this so much I bought one of the suggested racket handle attachments from etsy, which gives you the feel and weight of an actual tennis racket. the controller sits on the top of the racket handle, so the weight balance isn’t 100% accurate to a real racket, but it’s close enough. For almost everything, it’s a near 1:1 experience that doesn’t allow you to “cheat” by flicking your wrist instead of swinging with proper form, and you should be able to improve your real life tennis skills using it instead of developing bad controller-only muscle memory habits. 

There’s only one problem: the serve. 

The way the Quest works, the controllers are motion tracked with the cameras in the front of the headset. While this setup is perfect for 99% of VR games, when it comes to serving a tennis ball, proper serve form requires you to swing from a racket position behind your shoulder after the wind-up, ideally in one fluid two-part motion. 

The problem is, of course, that there are no tracking cameras on the back of the headset. What this means is that when you go to serve, the headset “loses” the position of your racket/controller, and then has to “re-find” it in the midst of an incredibly fast serve motion. Sometimes it’s fine, but it doesn’t always work — a lot of the time, you’ll go through a serve motion that would 100% make contact with the ball in real life, but because of this tracking issue, you wind up whiffing over and over again, and the result is that you start to train yourself to “cheat” the serve in order to retain controller tracking. 

I’m honestly not sure how the developers would even go about fixing this issue — I would imagine it’s purely a limitation of the headset tech itself, not the game. But it is annoying. 
 
But let’s be clear: in no way should this dissuade you from getting the game! Even just forehand training wise, it’s an invaluable purchase. (Devs, if you have any serve tips, I’d love to hear it — and excellent job overall!)

*****************

ORIGINAL REVIEW: I only got this game a week ago but as a lifelong tennis player, it’s shocking that it isn’t rated — at minimum — 4.8 stars. Personally, it’s 100/100. 
 
If the game hadn’t offered a free trial, I might have seen some of the bizarre reviews saying “it’s too difficult since they changed the formula” and never tried it. That would have been a crying shame. This game is amazing. Astonishingly amazing, actually. 
 
(I don’t know what the gameplay was like previously, but what I do know is that as of 12/6/24, on simulation mode, it is very nearly an EXACT replica of a real tennis match as far as the mechanics of form are concerned.)
 
And sure, tennis is difficult! If I hadn’t played before, I can imagine it might be irritating. But if you’re a tennis player, this is the game you want. It’s honestly a dream come true — the way the swing mechanics work with teleport, it perfectly sets you up in position for each shot, then it’s up to you to execute it, no matter how fast the ball is moving. It’s all reflexes and proper swing form — and it gives you the pure rush of all the best parts of a real life tennis match without any of the exhausting aspects. 

On that note, you know what’s even better? I have a TINY apartment, and I can still play this game to the fullest extent. 

BOY do I wish this technology had existed when I was a kid. Back in my day, to practice the perfect forehand serve return, your mom had to drive you to the tennis club and then you got maybe 15 minutes of actual drill time with 45 minutes of waiting around for your turn. With this program, it’s unlimited. It’s the purest swing practice that exists outside of a real life personal coach. 
 
The other crazy thing? Even though I’m not actually running around the court, I still work up a sweat every time. I’ve played it 2 hours a day every day since I bought it.
 
I already love this game so much I ordered a racket attachment (there’s a preset for certain controller attachments in the game) that’s supposed to mimic the weight and balance of a real life tennis racket perfectly. I’m waiting for it to get here and I’ll update this review after I try it. 
 
But long story short, this is easily one of the best $20 purchases I’ve ever made. If you played tennis, get it. Trust me.

There are no instructions that I can find, and no idea how to get it going. It keeps going back to a new profile. To make matters worse, both my husband and myself had to purchase the game. Total waste of money

developer_response.body: Please contact me at mikorigames@gmail.com. You should click on Continue on top of the main menu if you want to continue the world tour with the same profile. You can contact me on Discord too (https://discord.gg/Z3HN6g67Dy)

reviews.review_title: It does feel like the real thing
reviews.review_description: Indeed very realistic.  I wish more options for single player mode.  I don't use the game to play with other players online since I can do that on the real court everyday so all of my comment will target at the single player aspect.    I do wish to be able to  just practice receive serve which I don't think currently I can do that.   I also wish I can set some option to only have the bot hit the ball to either side so if I wanna practice just backhand strokes I can just do that.   The Mixed mode bot almost always hit just as same speed and height for every ball I wish there are some settings to add some varieties in that.   Is it possible also to have an option to replace the Mixed mode bot with an AI player instead?

developer_response.body: Mixed training is highly customizable. You can edit the list of every shot from the drone, adjusting each characteristic such as spin, speed, direction, and more. You can also train your return of serve by setting the return position so the game no longer moves you to the center of the court but to the service return spot. Contact me on Discord or at mikorigames@gmail.com if you need help.

reviews.review_description: Had problems with the app where the controller was dropping out of my hand, but that has since been fixed and it’s not working perfectly!￼

reviews.review_title: This is what VR is all about.
reviews.review_description:
The feeling, the physics, the sound. I love this game so much.

Please work on a graphics / textures update. You’d have the best game on Quest for sure.

reviews.review_title: Wow!! Best Physics Simulation Hands Down
reviews.review_description:
This is the first review I’ve written for an app on the Quest & it’s because it’s just so amazing. I started on version 4.8 and haven’t had any trouble bringing my tennis game to the app. I’ve been playing for about 30 minutes a day for the last 2 weeks & am able to compete with the AI on simulation (winning some and losing some). I lost the first 10 matches before grabbing one ahah. For context, I’m about a USTA 4.5. It’s a great game when rainy outdoors or if you want to get some extra practice. I picked up the HelloReal attachment to make my gameplay more like a real racquet as well. 

The physics aren’t PERFECT, but I’d say it’s like 90% of the way there (e.g. some serves I’d expect to go in the net end up going in). I’m excited to see how else the game keeps improving! I do wonder if things will get boring once I can consistently beat the AI, but as of now, I’m excited to play daily!! 

10/10!!! Way to go!
Really love this game, especially the training mode helps me a lot with my timing, ball tracking, contact point, and so on!

In version 4.7 this game was fun and challenging but, you could win some games. Now it’s no fun.

After talking with the developers, I’ve discovered their obnoxious and toxic response to constructive criticism is typical of arrogant elites. 

Do yourself a favor and avoid this game and any other by Mikori.

developer_response.body: Hi, I understand that you're used to the old physics from version 4.7, but the new, much more realistic physics has been enthusiastically received by 90% of the community, and the online matches in the World Championship tournaments confirm that the games are far more engaging and realistic, reflecting players' true skill. The same goes for arcade mode as well. For this reason, we won’t be going back to the old physics just for the 10% of players. We’re confident that, in time, they’ll come to appreciate it as well. If not, we're sorry, but FPT is a game in constant evolution toward the highest possible realism. Thank you for your support.

reviews.review_description: I love this game of course what is going to perfect in the VR as opposed to on court action outside. The only game  of tennis in VR, that I think is great 👍🏾 The only game of tennis for me, that comes close to this is playstation 3 move tennis. Only because no headset needed, on the Sports Champions disc. It is the only tennis game  that can compare to FPT, in my opinion. I played that until VR FPT came along, none of them are perfect but they come closer for me than any other tennis games 👌🏾👌🏾🎾😊 thanks Mikori games great product.

FPT is by far the best vr tennis. The physics👌🏻 They have professional tennis players playing and even they praise it. Arcade is like a boon to all non tennis players who dream of playing such skilled tennis but can’t in real life. FPT arcade is the place where you could show off your tennis mind.

Don’t buy the haters here as they are all old players who no longer could keep winning when the physics changed to skill based. If you are a new player, this wouldn’t concern you at all.
Also, the developer is quick in answering your concerns and open to suggestions.

No way, those fancy looking tennis games out there would ever be able to match the realness that this game offers.

For the love of God add weight to the rackets! It is a great game but I too often find myself trying to swing the racket as hard as I do in real life but since there is no weight the racket does not follow behind as it should and so my timing becomes off.
Other than that it is a great game and ya’ll have done a great job at creating the physics just please add some weight.

developer_response.body: If you want to add weight, you should buy a tennis adapter that simulates the weight of a real racket. Feel free to contact me at mikorigames@gmail.com, and I’ll be happy to help you.

reviews.review_description:
Update:
I’m not sure why the developer decides to make excuses instead of fixing the issues with the game!
The ball bouncing twice isn’t the only issue!! What about the ball not getting hit by the rackets?!! I’ve tried calibrating the racket and tried different ways to hold the controller!!!
Still… the ball gets missed 5 out of ten times !!!
How is that acceptable ??
What about the bad calls for the ball clearly being out!!! Sometimes in my favor and sometimes against me!!!!
I’m not the only one who experiences these issues !!! Other players have complained about the same issues but calls for resolving the issues are always ignored.
How about fixing the bugs players are experiencing with this game instead of just blaming it on network lag or whatever else you want to blame it on?!
We’ve paid for a broken game that the developer refuses to fix but instead blames it on other issues !!!
Completely unfair and ridiculous to say the least !!


Original review: 
I’ve had this game for about three weeks now and I honestly regret buying it.
The game’s online mode is absolutely useless. In the online mode, when I hit the ball to the opponent’s side, the ball bounces twice on the opponent’s side and he still manages to get to it and hit back to my side!!!!
I’m not the only one who experienced this bug. This happens in almost every single play during the game. 
It doesn’t just happen once during the game, it happens in every single play during the game!!!
Other users have complained about this issue and it was brushed off as a minor issue due to network lags.
It literally ruins the experience. 
I do not recommend this game… avoid this game and look for better alternatives with better experience and better graphics. It is way overrated!

developer_response.body:
Because they are NOT bugs.
1. You see the ball bouncing twice due to internet lags. The opponent hits the ball after the first bouce but the input of the shots takes time to reach you, so meanwhile you see the ball bouncing twice. To avoid this just select a better server, play with an opponent with a better ping or activate the pingball option.
2. If you miss the ball could be your fault or loss of tracking (the headset cannot see the controller correctly)
3. If you have a doubt about a call, raise your hand and press trigger to call the hawkeye.
If you want more help join Discord. We play online tournaments every month woth money prizes. Next month there are the FPT Finals.

reviews.review_description: Thought I had a problem but after talking to the developer it appears that it’s ok I was using the wrong method. Making this game as I thought before the best tennis game I’ve played. Well worth the purchase!

developer_response.body: Contact me at mikorigames@gmail.com

reviews.review_description: The best tennis sport EVER! I have lost some  weight playing this game added to other sport I play.  I have several tennis apps and none of them reach the level as this one.

reviews.review_title: Feels like you're playing real tennis !
reviews.review_description: I feels so real and very rewarding when you start to get more confidence and play rally after rally. Excellent way to get fit. Fantastic !
fun to play 🔥

Love this game; I usually play it during the winter as indoor tennis is ridiculously expensive so this is a great alternative. After one of the updates; my forehand has been feeling a little strange; I'm suddenly adding 3000 top spin and a lot of my forehands are coming up short and into the net whereas when I first started the game; this didn't happen.

reviews.review_title: Awesome on Quest 1, but bad on Quest 3
reviews.review_description: It is great on Quest 1, very close to real tennis. I enjoy it so much and play it all the time. Once I upgrade device to Quest 3, it becomes unplayable. The ball is slow, racket feels like a sponge, the ball no longer goes like Quest 1 before, and the racket miss the ball a lot.

developer_response.body: Press B/Y during a match or training, click on controller calibration and click on Default. Contact me at mikorigames@gmail.com if you need more help

reviews.review_description: This used to be one of the best games out there. After some updates to the game, it has become very frustrating to play. This used to be so much fun for those of us who aren’t tennis pro’s. To make things worse the developers ask for feedback on their updates. If your feedback is negative, they will threaten to ban you from their Discord channel if they don’t like your feedback. IF you do provide feedback, their first instinct is to tell you that the problem is YOU and not their changes.

developer_response.body: I see that you didn’t understand the meaning of the message on Discord or what I’m doing at all. I invite you to read it again

reviews.review_description: Ok, let me say, first off, that I love this game. I’ve been playing it for over a year, and it is the closest thing I’ve found to actually playing tennis.  And while it’s always been great, the new PRO level in v4.8 definitely kicks it up a notch.  (Importantly, you can adjust the settings to make it fun for anyone, regardless of skill.)  In the prior version I was consistently beating the AI opponent, many times 3-0.  But with the new release, not only is the AI opponent far better (fewer errors/predictable shots) but - more importantly - the improved physics means that any lazy habits I was getting away with in the prior version no longer work. With the PRO setting you really need to swing correctly for your shots to be effective.   To make it even more like real tennis I use the VR Sports Grip from Virtual Printality and add weight to it, so the controller has a swing feel closer to my actual racket.   Highly, highly recommended.

reviews.review_title: Was a great game for general fitness
reviews.review_description: I bought the Quest 3 in Nov 2023 just for fitness and fun. After a free trial of First Tennis I was sold on the whole Meta VR experience. I went on to purchase this game which was great until the 4.8 update. Unless your pro player the game is just useless. I wish Meta would offer a refund on games developer updates  spoils the game. I cancelled my Meta Plus subscription today.

developer_response.body: Hi, the new update is more realistic and removes some shot assists. This change was made because many players complained that the arcade mode was too automatic, allowing some players to ruin matches by just touching the ball and making automatic winners. Now, the trajectory is 90% assisted, but power is not assisted, so you need to calibrate the power manually. This way, the match depends more on your actual tennis skills. After some time to adjust, you will love this update, just like the entire FPT community did.

reviews.review_title: Love tennis but not this one anymore…
reviews.review_description:
Since I can no longer play real tennis, I do enjoy this game-for the most part. Only complaint is that the teleportation isn’t quite accurate and I’m not 6’ tall!

Update: With each update the game is getting worse. It’s impossible to enjoy a game I once loved IRL and now can’t enjoy in VR. ☹️

Update: Thanks to the amazing support of Mikori Games I made the changes they advised and voile I am enjoying playing tennis again!!! Thank you so much!

Worst update EVER!! Cleared app data and it is still horrible! Time to free up space on my headset for something a LOT better…….
